     106              : typedef struct OnCommitItem
     107              : {
     108              :         Oid                     relid;                  /* relid of relation */
     109              :         OnCommitAction oncommit;        /* what to do at end of xact */
     110              : 
     111              :         /*
     112              :          * If this entry was created during the current transaction,
     113              :          * creating_subid is the ID of the creating subxact; if created in a prior
     114              :          * transaction, creating_subid is zero.  If deleted during the current
     115              :          * transaction, deleting_subid is the ID of the deleting subxact; if no
     116              :          * deletion request is pending, deleting_subid is zero.
     117              :          */
     118              :         SubTransactionId creating_subid;
     119              :         SubTransactionId deleting_subid;
     120              : } OnCommitItem;
     121              : 
     122              : static List *on_commits = NIL;
     123              : 
     124              : 
     125              : /*
     126              :  * State information for ALTER TABLE
     127              :  *
     128              :  * The pending-work queue for an ALTER TABLE is a List of AlteredTableInfo
     129              :  * structs, one for each table modified by the operation (the named table
     130              :  * plus any child tables that are affected).  We save lists of subcommands
     131              :  * to apply to this table (possibly modified by parse transformation steps);
     132              :  * these lists will be executed in Phase 2.  If a Phase 3 step is needed,
     133              :  * necessary information is stored in the constraints and newvals lists.
     134              :  *
     135              :  * Phase 2 is divided into multiple passes; subcommands are executed in
     136              :  * a pass determined by subcommand type.
     137              :  */
     138              : 
     139              : #define AT_PASS_UNSET                   -1      /* UNSET will cause ERROR */
     140              : #define AT_PASS_DROP                    0       /* DROP (all flavors) */
     141              : #define AT_PASS_ALTER_TYPE              1       /* ALTER COLUMN TYPE */
     142              : #define AT_PASS_OLD_INDEX               2       /* re-add existing indexes */
     143              : #define AT_PASS_OLD_CONSTR              3       /* re-add existing constraints */
     144              : /* We could support a RENAME COLUMN pass here, but not currently used */
     145              : #define AT_PASS_ADD_COL                 4       /* ADD COLUMN */
     146              : #define AT_PASS_ADD_CONSTR              5       /* ADD constraints (initial examination) */
     147              : #define AT_PASS_COL_ATTRS               6       /* set column attributes, eg NOT NULL */
     148              : #define AT_PASS_ADD_INDEXCONSTR 7       /* ADD index-based constraints */
     149              : #define AT_PASS_ADD_INDEX               8       /* ADD indexes */
     150              : #define AT_PASS_ADD_OTHERCONSTR 9       /* ADD other constraints, defaults */
     151              : #define AT_PASS_MISC                    10      /* other stuff */
     152              : #define AT_NUM_PASSES                   11
     153              : 
     154              : typedef struct AlteredTableInfo
     155              : {
     156              :         /* Information saved before any work commences: */
     157              :         Oid                     relid;                  /* Relation to work on */
     158              :         char            relkind;                /* Its relkind */
     159              :         TupleDesc       oldDesc;                /* Pre-modification tuple descriptor */
     160              :         /* Information saved by Phase 1 for Phase 2: */
     161              :         List       *subcmds[AT_NUM_PASSES]; /* Lists of AlterTableCmd */
     162              :         /* Information saved by Phases 1/2 for Phase 3: */
     163              :         List       *constraints;        /* List of NewConstraint */
     164              :         List       *newvals;            /* List of NewColumnValue */
     165              :         List       *afterStmts;         /* List of utility command parsetrees */
     166              :         bool            verify_new_notnull; /* T if we should recheck NOT NULL */
     167              :         int                     rewrite;                /* Reason for forced rewrite, if any */
     168              :         Oid                     newTableSpace;  /* new tablespace; 0 means no change */
     169              :         bool            chgPersistence; /* T if SET LOGGED/UNLOGGED is used */
     170              :         char            newrelpersistence;      /* if above is true */
     171              :         Expr       *partition_constraint;       /* for attach partition validation */
     172              :         /* true, if validating default due to some other attach/detach */
     173              :         bool            validate_default;
     174              :         /* Objects to rebuild after completing ALTER TYPE operations */
     175              :         List       *changedConstraintOids;      /* OIDs of constraints to rebuild */
     176              :         List       *changedConstraintDefs;      /* string definitions of same */
     177              :         List       *changedIndexOids;   /* OIDs of indexes to rebuild */
     178              :         List       *changedIndexDefs;   /* string definitions of same */
     179              :         char       *replicaIdentityIndex;       /* index to reset as REPLICA IDENTITY */
     180              :         char       *clusterOnIndex; /* index to use for CLUSTER */
     181              : } AlteredTableInfo;
     182              : 
     183              : /* Struct describing one new constraint to check in Phase 3 scan */
     184              : /* Note: new NOT NULL constraints are handled elsewhere */
     185              : typedef struct NewConstraint
     186              : {
     187              :         char       *name;                       /* Constraint name, or NULL if none */
     188              :         ConstrType      contype;                /* CHECK or FOREIGN */
     189              :         Oid                     refrelid;               /* PK rel, if FOREIGN */
     190              :         Oid                     refindid;               /* OID of PK's index, if FOREIGN */
     191              :         Oid                     conid;                  /* OID of pg_constraint entry, if FOREIGN */
     192              :         Node       *qual;                       /* Check expr or CONSTR_FOREIGN Constraint */
     193              :         ExprState  *qualstate;          /* Execution state for CHECK expr */
     194              : } NewConstraint;
     195              : 
     196              : /*
     197              :  * Struct describing one new column value that needs to be computed during
     198              :  * Phase 3 copy (this could be either a new column with a non-null default, or
     199              :  * a column that we're changing the type of).  Columns without such an entry
     200              :  * are just copied from the old table during ATRewriteTable.  Note that the
     201              :  * expr is an expression over *old* table values, except when is_generated
     202              :  * is true; then it is an expression over columns of the *new* tuple.
     203              :  */
     204              : typedef struct NewColumnValue
     205              : {
     206              :         AttrNumber      attnum;                 /* which column */
     207              :         Expr       *expr;                       /* expression to compute */
     208              :         ExprState  *exprstate;          /* execution state */
     209              :         bool            is_generated;   /* is it a GENERATED expression? */
     210              : } NewColumnValue;

     306              : /*
     307              :  * Partition tables are expected to be dropped when the parent partitioned
     308              :  * table gets dropped. Hence for partitioning we use AUTO dependency.
     309              :  * Otherwise, for regular inheritance use NORMAL dependency.
     310              :  */
     311              : #define child_dependency_type(child_is_partition)       \
     312              :         ((child_is_partition) ? DEPENDENCY_AUTO : DEPENDENCY_NORMAL)

     574              : /* ----------------------------------------------------------------
     575              :  *              DefineRelation
     576              :  *                              Creates a new relation.
     577              :  *
     578              :  * stmt carries parsetree information from an ordinary CREATE TABLE statement.
     579              :  * The other arguments are used to extend the behavior for other cases:
     580              :  * relkind: relkind to assign to the new relation
     581              :  * ownerId: if not InvalidOid, use this as the new relation's owner.
     582              :  * typaddress: if not null, it's set to the pg_type entry's address.
     583              :  * queryString: for error reporting
     584              :  *
     585              :  * Note that permissions checks are done against current user regardless of
     586              :  * ownerId.  A nonzero ownerId is used when someone is creating a relation
     587              :  * "on behalf of" someone else, so we still want to see that the current user
     588              :  * has permissions to do it.
     589              :  *
     590              :  * If successful, returns the address of the new relation.
     591              :  * ----------------------------------------------------------------
     592              :  */

     641              :         else
     642          345 :                 partitioned = false;
     643              : 
     644              :         /*

     650              :         namespaceId =
     651          371 :                 RangeVarGetAndCheckCreationNamespace(stmt->relation, NoLock, NULL);
     652              : 
     653              :         /*
     654              :          * Security check: disallow creating temp tables from security-restricted

     657              :          */

     660            0 :                 ereport(ERROR,
     661              :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_INSUFFICIENT_PRIVILEGE),
     662              :                                  errmsg("cannot create temporary table within security-restricted operation")));
     663              : 
     664              :         /*
     665              :          * Determine the lockmode to use when scanning parents.  A self-exclusive
     666              :          * lock is needed here.
     667              :          *
     668              :          * For regular inheritance, if two backends attempt to add children to the
     669              :          * same parent simultaneously, and that parent has no pre-existing
     670              :          * children, then both will attempt to update the parent's relhassubclass
     671              :          * field, leading to a "tuple concurrently updated" error.  Also, this
     672              :          * interlocks against a concurrent ANALYZE on the parent table, which
     673              :          * might otherwise be attempting to clear the parent's relhassubclass
     674              :          * field, if its previous children were recently dropped.

    1572              : /*
    1573              :  * ExecuteTruncate
    1574              :  *              Executes a TRUNCATE command.
    1575              :  *
    1576              :  * This is a multi-relation truncate.  We first open and grab exclusive

    2088              : /*----------
    2089              :  * MergeAttributes
    2090              :  *              Returns new schema given initial schema and superclasses.
    2091              :  *
    2092              :  * Input arguments:
    2093              :  * 'schema' is the column/attribute definition for the table. (It's a list
    2094              :  *              of ColumnDef's.) It is destructively changed.
    2095              :  * 'supers' is a list of OIDs of parent relations, already locked by caller.
    2096              :  * 'relpersistence' is the persistence type of the table.
    2097              :  * 'is_partition' tells if the table is a partition.
    2098              :  *
    2099              :  * Output arguments:
    2100              :  * 'supconstr' receives a list of constraints belonging to the parents,
    2101              :  *              updated as necessary to be valid for the child.
    2102              :  *
    2103              :  * Return value:
    2104              :  * Completed schema list.
    2105              :  *
    2106              :  * Notes:
    2107              :  *        The order in which the attributes are inherited is very important.
    2108              :  *        Intuitively, the inherited attributes should come first. If a table
    2109              :  *        inherits from multiple parents, the order of those attributes are
    2110              :  *        according to the order of the parents specified in CREATE TABLE.
    2111              :  *
    2112              :  *        Here's an example:
    2113              :  *
    2114              :  *              create table person (name text, age int4, location point);
    2115              :  *              create table emp (salary int4, manager text) inherits(person);
    2116              :  *              create table student (gpa float8) inherits (person);
    2117              :  *              create table stud_emp (percent int4) inherits (emp, student);
    2118              :  *
    2119              :  *        The order of the attributes of stud_emp is:
    2120              :  *
    2121              :  *                                                      person {1:name, 2:age, 3:location}
    2122              :  *                                                      /        \
    2123              :  *                         {6:gpa}      student   emp {4:salary, 5:manager}
    2124              :  *                                                      \        /
    2125              :  *                                                 stud_emp {7:percent}
    2126              :  *
    2127              :  *         If the same attribute name appears multiple times, then it appears
    2128              :  *         in the result table in the proper location for its first appearance.
    2129              :  *
    2130              :  *         Constraints (including NOT NULL constraints) for the child table
    2131              :  *         are the union of all relevant constraints, from both the child schema
    2132              :  *         and parent tables.
    2133              :  *
    2134              :  *         The default value for a child column is defined as:
    2135              :  *              (1) If the child schema specifies a default, that value is used.
    2136              :  *              (2) If neither the child nor any parent specifies a default, then
    2137              :  *                      the column will not have a default.
    2138              :  *              (3) If conflicting defaults are inherited from different parents
    2139              :  *                      (and not overridden by the child), an error is raised.
    2140              :  *              (4) Otherwise the inherited default is used.
    2141              :  *              Rule (3) is new in Postgres 7.1; in earlier releases you got a
    2142              :  *              rather arbitrary choice of which parent default to use.
    2143              :  *----------
    2144              :  */
    2145              : static List *
    2146          371 : MergeAttributes(List *schema, List *supers, char relpersistence,
    2147              :                                 bool is_partition, List **supconstr)
    2148              : {
    2149          371 :         List       *inhSchema = NIL;
    2150          371 :         List       *constraints = NIL;
    2151          371 :         bool            have_bogus_defaults = false;
    2152              :         int                     child_attno;
    2153              :         static Node bogus_marker = {0}; /* marks conflicting defaults */
    2154          371 :         List       *saved_schema = NIL;
    2155              :         ListCell   *entry;
    2156              : 
    2157              :         /*
    2158              :          * Check for and reject tables with too many columns. We perform this
    2159              :          * check relatively early for two reasons: (a) we don't run the risk of
    2160              :          * overflowing an AttrNumber in subsequent code (b) an O(n^2) algorithm is
    2161              :          * okay if we're processing <= 1600 columns, but could take minutes to
    2162              :          * execute if the user attempts to create a table with hundreds of
    2163              :          * thousands of columns.
    2164              :          *
    2165              :          * Note that we also need to check that we do not exceed this figure after
    2166              :          * including columns from inherited relations.
    2167              :          */
    2168          371 :         if (list_length(schema) > MaxHeapAttributeNumber)
    2169            0 :                 ereport(ERROR,
    2170              :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_TOO_MANY_COLUMNS),
    2171              :                                  errmsg("tables can have at most %d columns",
    2172              :                                                 MaxHeapAttributeNumber)));
    2173              : 
    2174              :         /*
    2175              :          * Check for duplicate names in the explicit list of attributes.

    2177              :          * Although we might consider merging such entries in the same way that we

    2179              :          * sense to assume such conflicts are errors.
    2180              :          *
    2181              :          * We don't use foreach() here because we have two nested loops over the

    2233              :                 }

    2857              : 
    2858              : /*
    2859              :  * MergeCheckConstraint
    2860              :  *              Try to merge an inherited CHECK constraint with previous ones
    2861              :  *
    2862              :  * If we inherit identically-named constraints from multiple parents, we must
    2863              :  * merge them, or throw an error if they don't have identical definitions.
    2864              :  *
    2865              :  * constraints is a list of CookedConstraint structs for previous constraints.
    2866              :  *
    2867              :  * Returns true if merged (constraint is a duplicate), or false if it's
    2868              :  * got a so-far-unique name, or throws error if conflict.
    2869              :  */
    2870              : static bool
    2871            0 : MergeCheckConstraint(List *constraints, char *name, Node *expr)
    2872              : {
    2873              :         ListCell   *lc;
    2874              : 
    2875            0 :         foreach(lc, constraints)
    2876              :         {
    2877            0 :                 CookedConstraint *ccon = (CookedConstraint *) lfirst(lc);
    2878              : 
    2879              :                 Assert(ccon->contype == CONSTR_CHECK);
    2880              : 
    2881              :                 /* Non-matching names never conflict */
    2882            0 :                 if (strcmp(ccon->name, name) != 0)
    2883            0 :                         continue;
    2884              : 
    2885            0 :                 if (equal(expr, ccon->expr))
    2886              :                 {
    2887              :                         /* OK to merge */
    2888            0 :                         ccon->inhcount++;
    2889            0 :                         return true;
    2890              :                 }

    3016              : /*
    3017              :  * SetRelationHasSubclass
    3018              :  *              Set the value of the relation's relhassubclass field in pg_class.
    3019              :  *
    3020              :  * It's always safe to set this field to true, because all SQL commands are
    3021              :  * ready to see true and then find no children.  On the other hand, commands
    3022              :  * generally assume zero children if this is false.
    3023              :  *
    3024              :  * Caller must hold any self-exclusive lock until end of transaction.  If the
    3025              :  * new value is false, caller must have acquired that lock before reading the
    3026              :  * evidence that justified the false value.  That way, it properly waits if
    3027              :  * another backend is simultaneously concluding no need to change the tuple
    3028              :  * (new and old values are true).
    3029              :  *
    3030              :  * NOTE: an important side-effect of this operation is that an SI invalidation
    3031              :  * message is sent out to all backends --- including me --- causing plans
    3032              :  * referencing the relation to be rebuilt with the new list of children.
    3033              :  * This must happen even if we find that no change is needed in the pg_class
    3034              :  * row.
    3035              :  */
    3036              : void
    3037           71 : SetRelationHasSubclass(Oid relationId, bool relhassubclass)
    3038              : {
    3039              :         Relation        relationRelation;
    3040              :         HeapTuple       tuple;
    3041              :         Form_pg_class classtuple;
    3042              : 
    3043              :         Assert(CheckRelationOidLockedByMe(relationId,
    3044              :                                                                           ShareUpdateExclusiveLock, false) ||

    3046              :                                                                           ShareRowExclusiveLock, true));
    3047              : 
    3048              :         /*
    3049              :          * Fetch a modifiable copy of the tuple, modify it, update pg_class.
    3050              :          */
    3051           71 :         relationRelation = table_open(RelationRelationId, RowExclusiveLock);
    3052           71 :         tuple = SearchSysCacheCopy1(RELOID, ObjectIdGetDatum(relationId));
    3053           71 :         if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tuple))
    3054            0 :                 elog(ERROR, "cache lookup failed for relation %u", relationId);
    3055           71 :         classtuple = (Form_pg_class) GETSTRUCT(tuple);
    3056              : 
    3057           71 :         if (classtuple->relhassubclass != relhassubclass)
    3058              :         {
    3059           43 :                 classtuple->relhassubclass = relhassubclass;
    3060           43 :                 CatalogTupleUpdate(relationRelation, &tuple->t_self, tuple);
    3061              :         }
    3062              :         else
    3063              :         {
    3064              :                 /* no need to change tuple, but force relcache rebuild anyway */
    3065           28 :                 CacheInvalidateRelcacheByTuple(tuple);
    3066              :         }
    3067              : 
    3068           71 :         heap_freetuple(tuple);
    3069           71 :         table_close(relationRelation, RowExclusiveLock);
    3070           71 : }

    3392              : /*
    3393              :  *              renameatt               - changes the name of an attribute in a relation

    3776              : 
    3777              : /*
    3778              :  * Disallow ALTER TABLE (and similar commands) when the current backend has
    3779              :  * any open reference to the target table besides the one just acquired by
    3780              :  * the calling command; this implies there's an open cursor or active plan.
    3781              :  * We need this check because our lock doesn't protect us against stomping
    3782              :  * on our own foot, only other people's feet!
    3783              :  *
    3784              :  * For ALTER TABLE, the only case known to cause serious trouble is ALTER
    3785              :  * COLUMN TYPE, and some changes are obviously pretty benign, so this could
    3786              :  * possibly be relaxed to only error out for certain types of alterations.
    3787              :  * But the use-case for allowing any of these things is not obvious, so we
    3788              :  * won't work hard at it for now.
    3789              :  *
    3790              :  * We also reject these commands if there are any pending AFTER trigger events
    3791              :  * for the rel.  This is certainly necessary for the rewriting variants of
    3792              :  * ALTER TABLE, because they don't preserve tuple TIDs and so the pending
    3793              :  * events would try to fetch the wrong tuples.  It might be overly cautious
    3794              :  * in other cases, but again it seems better to err on the side of paranoia.
    3795              :  *
    3796              :  * REINDEX calls this with "rel" referencing the index to be rebuilt; here
    3797              :  * we are worried about active indexscans on the index.  The trigger-event
    3798              :  * check can be skipped, since we are doing no damage to the parent table.
    3799              :  *
    3800              :  * The statement name (eg, "ALTER TABLE") is passed for use in error messages.
    3801              :  */
    3802              : void
    3803          584 : CheckTableNotInUse(Relation rel, const char *stmt)
    3804              : {
    3805              :         int                     expected_refcnt;
    3806              : 
    3807          584 :         expected_refcnt = rel->rd_isnailed ? 2 : 1;
    3808          584 :         if (rel->rd_refcnt != expected_refcnt)
    3809            0 :                 ereport(ERROR,
    3810              :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_OBJECT_IN_USE),
    3811              :                 /* translator: first %s is a SQL command, eg ALTER TABLE */
    3812              :                                  errmsg("cannot %s \"%s\" because it is being used by active queries in this session",
    3813              :                                                 stmt, RelationGetRelationName(rel))));
    3814              : 
    3815          584 :         if (rel->rd_rel->relkind != RELKIND_INDEX &&
    3816          922 :                 rel->rd_rel->relkind != RELKIND_PARTITIONED_INDEX &&
    3817          452 :                 AfterTriggerPendingOnRel(RelationGetRelid(rel)))
    3818            0 :                 ereport(ERROR,
    3819              :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_OBJECT_IN_USE),
    3820              :                 /* translator: first %s is a SQL command, eg ALTER TABLE */
    3821              :                                  errmsg("cannot %s \"%s\" because it has pending trigger events",
    3822              :                                                 stmt, RelationGetRelationName(rel))));
    3823          584 : }
    3824              : 
    3825              : /*
    3826              :  * CheckAlterTableIsSafe
    3827              :  *              Verify that it's safe to allow ALTER TABLE on this relation.
    3828              :  *
    3829              :  * This consists of CheckTableNotInUse() plus a check that the relation
    3830              :  * isn't another session's temp table.  We must split out the temp-table
    3831              :  * check because there are callers of CheckTableNotInUse() that don't want
    3832              :  * that, notably DROP TABLE.  (We must allow DROP or we couldn't clean out
    3833              :  * an orphaned temp schema.)  Compare truncate_check_activity().
    3834              :  */
    3835              : static void
    3836           59 : CheckAlterTableIsSafe(Relation rel)
    3837              : {
    3838              :         /*
    3839              :          * Don't allow ALTER on temp tables of other backends.  Their local buffer
    3840              :          * manager is not going to cope if we need to change the table's contents.
    3841              :          * Even if we don't, there may be optimizations that assume temp tables
    3842              :          * aren't subject to such interference.
    3843              :          */
    3844           59 :         if (RELATION_IS_OTHER_TEMP(rel))
    3845            0 :                 ereport(ERROR,
    3846              :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
    3847              :                                  errmsg("cannot alter temporary tables of other sessions")));
    3848              : 
    3849              :         /*
    3850              :          * Also check for active uses of the relation in the current transaction,
    3851              :          * including open scans and pending AFTER trigger events.
    3852              :          */
    3853           59 :         CheckTableNotInUse(rel, "ALTER TABLE");
    3854           59 : }
    3855              : 
    3856              : /*
    3857              :  * AlterTableLookupRelation
    3858              :  *              Look up, and lock, the OID for the relation named by an alter table
    3859              :  *              statement.
    3860              :  */
    3861              : Oid
    3862           56 : AlterTableLookupRelation(AlterTableStmt *stmt, LOCKMODE lockmode)
    3863              : {
    3864          112 :         return RangeVarGetRelidExtended(stmt->relation, lockmode,
    3865           56 :                                                                         stmt->missing_ok ? RVR_MISSING_OK : 0,
    3866              :                                                                         RangeVarCallbackForAlterRelation,
    3867              :                                                                         (void *) stmt);
    3868              : }
    3869              : 
    3870              : /*
    3871              :  * AlterTable
    3872              :  *              Execute ALTER TABLE, which can be a list of subcommands
    3873              :  *
    3874              :  * ALTER TABLE is performed in three phases:
    3875              :  *              1. Examine subcommands and perform pre-transformation checking.
    3876              :  *              2. Validate and transform subcommands, and update system catalogs.
    3877              :  *              3. Scan table(s) to check new constraints, and optionally recopy
    3878              :  *                 the data into new table(s).
    3879              :  * Phase 3 is not performed unless one or more of the subcommands requires
    3880              :  * it.  The intention of this design is to allow multiple independent
    3881              :  * updates of the table schema to be performed with only one pass over the
    3882              :  * data.
    3883              :  *
    3884              :  * ATPrepCmd performs phase 1.  A "work queue" entry is created for
    3885              :  * each table to be affected (there may be multiple affected tables if the
    3886              :  * commands traverse a table inheritance hierarchy).  Also we do preliminary
    3887              :  * validation of the subcommands.  Because earlier subcommands may change
    3888              :  * the catalog state seen by later commands, there are limits to what can
    3889              :  * be done in this phase.  Generally, this phase acquires table locks,
    3890              :  * checks permissions and relkind, and recurses to find child tables.
    3891              :  *
    3892              :  * ATRewriteCatalogs performs phase 2 for each affected table.
    3893              :  * Certain subcommands need to be performed before others to avoid
    3894              :  * unnecessary conflicts; for example, DROP COLUMN should come before
    3895              :  * ADD COLUMN.  Therefore phase 1 divides the subcommands into multiple
    3896              :  * lists, one for each logical "pass" of phase 2.
    3897              :  *
    3898              :  * ATRewriteTables performs phase 3 for those tables that need it.
    3899              :  *
    3900              :  * For most subcommand types, phases 2 and 3 do no explicit recursion,
    3901              :  * since phase 1 already does it.  However, for certain subcommand types
    3902              :  * it is only possible to determine how to recurse at phase 2 time; for
    3903              :  * those cases, phase 1 sets the cmd->recurse flag (or, in some older coding,
    3904              :  * changes the command subtype of a "Recurse" variant XXX to be cleaned up.)
    3905              :  *
    3906              :  * Thanks to the magic of MVCC, an error anywhere along the way rolls back
    3907              :  * the whole operation; we don't have to do anything special to clean up.
    3908              :  *
    3909              :  * The caller must lock the relation, with an appropriate lock level
    3910              :  * for the subcommands requested, using AlterTableGetLockLevel(stmt->cmds)
    3911              :  * or higher. We pass the lock level down
    3912              :  * so that we can apply it recursively to inherited tables. Note that the
    3913              :  * lock level we want as we recurse might well be higher than required for
    3914              :  * that specific subcommand. So we pass down the overall lock requirement,
    3915              :  * rather than reassess it at lower levels.
    3916              :  *
    3917              :  * The caller also provides a "context" which is to be passed back to
    3918              :  * utility.c when we need to execute a subcommand such as CREATE INDEX.
    3919              :  * Some of the fields therein, such as the relid, are used here as well.
    3920              :  */
    3921              : void
    3922           56 : AlterTable(AlterTableStmt *stmt, LOCKMODE lockmode,
    3923              :                    AlterTableUtilityContext *context)
    3924              : {
    3925              :         Relation        rel;
    3926              : 
    3927              :         /* Caller is required to provide an adequate lock. */
    3928           56 :         rel = relation_open(context->relid, NoLock);
    3929              : 
    3930           56 :         CheckAlterTableIsSafe(rel);
    3931              : 
    3932           56 :         ATController(stmt, rel, stmt->cmds, stmt->relation->inh, lockmode, context);
    3933           51 : }
    3934              : 
    3935              : /*
    3936              :  * AlterTableInternal
    3937              :  *
    3938              :  * ALTER TABLE with target specified by OID
    3939              :  *
    3940              :  * We do not reject if the relation is already open, because it's quite
    3941              :  * likely that one or more layers of caller have it open.  That means it
    3942              :  * is unsafe to use this entry point for alterations that could break
    3943              :  * existing query plans.  On the assumption it's not used for such, we
    3944              :  * don't have to reject pending AFTER triggers, either.
    3945              :  *
    3946              :  * Also, since we don't have an AlterTableUtilityContext, this cannot be
    3947              :  * used for any subcommand types that require parse transformation or
    3948              :  * could generate subcommands that have to be passed to ProcessUtility.
    3949              :  */

    3951            0 : AlterTableInternal(Oid relid, List *cmds, bool recurse)
    3952              : {
    3953              :         Relation        rel;

    3955              : 
    3956            0 :         rel = relation_open(relid, lockmode);
    3957              : 
    3958            0 :         EventTriggerAlterTableRelid(relid);
    3959              : 
    3960            0 :         ATController(NULL, rel, cmds, recurse, lockmode, NULL);
    3961            0 : }

    3963              : /*
    3964              :  * AlterTableGetLockLevel
    3965              :  *
    3966              :  * Sets the overall lock level required for the supplied list of subcommands.
    3967              :  * Policy for doing this set according to needs of AlterTable(), see
    3968              :  * comments there for overall explanation.
    3969              :  *
    3970              :  * Function is called before and after parsing, so it must give same
    3971              :  * answer each time it is called. Some subcommands are transformed
    3972              :  * into other subcommand types, so the transform must never be made to a
    3973              :  * lower lock level than previously assigned. All transforms are noted below.
    3974              :  *
    3975              :  * Since this is called before we lock the table we cannot use table metadata
    3976              :  * to influence the type of lock we acquire.
    3977              :  *
    3978              :  * There should be no lockmodes hardcoded into the subcommand functions. All
    3979              :  * lockmode decisions for ALTER TABLE are made here only. The one exception is
    3980              :  * ALTER TABLE RENAME which is treated as a different statement type T_RenameStmt
    3981              :  * and does not travel through this section of code and cannot be combined with
    3982              :  * any of the subcommands given here.
    3983              :  *
    3984              :  * Note that Hot Standby only knows about AccessExclusiveLocks on the master
    3985              :  * so any changes that might affect SELECTs running on standbys need to use
    3986              :  * AccessExclusiveLocks even if you think a lesser lock would do, unless you
    3987              :  * have a solution for that also.
    3988              :  *
    3989              :  * Also note that pg_dump uses only an AccessShareLock, meaning that anything
    3990              :  * that takes a lock less than AccessExclusiveLock can change object definitions
    3991              :  * while pg_dump is running. Be careful to check that the appropriate data is
    3992              :  * derived by pg_dump using an MVCC snapshot, rather than syscache lookups,
    3993              :  * otherwise we might end up with an inconsistent dump that can't restore.
    3994              :  */

    4619              : /*
    4620              :  * ATRewriteCatalogs
    4621              :  *
    4622              :  * Traffic cop for ALTER TABLE Phase 2 operations.  Subcommands are
    4623              :  * dispatched in a "safe" execution order (designed to avoid unnecessary
    4624              :  * conflicts).
    4625              :  */
    4626              : static void
    4627           54 : ATRewriteCatalogs(List **wqueue, LOCKMODE lockmode,

    4631              :         ListCell   *ltab;
    4632              : 
    4633              :         /*
    4634              :          * We process all the tables "in parallel", one pass at a time.  This is
    4635              :          * needed because we may have to propagate work from one table to another
    4636              :          * (specifically, ALTER TYPE on a foreign key's PK has to dispatch the
    4637              :          * re-adding of the foreign key constraint to the other table).  Work can
    4638              :          * only be propagated into later passes, however.
    4639              :          */

    4641              :         {
    4642              :                 /* Go through each table that needs to be processed */

    4647              :                         Relation        rel;
    4648              :                         ListCell   *lcmd;
    4649              : 
    4650          608 :                         if (subcmds == NIL)
    4651          524 :                                 continue;
    4652              : 
    4653              :                         /*
    4654              :                          * Appropriate lock was obtained by phase 1, needn't get it again
    4655              :                          */
    4656           84 :                         rel = relation_open(tab->relid, NoLock);
    4657              : 
    4658          166 :                         foreach(lcmd, subcmds)

    4670              :

    4673              :         }

    5024              : /*
    5025              :  * ATParseTransformCmd: perform parse transformation for one subcommand
    5026              :  *
    5027              :  * Returns the transformed subcommand tree, if there is one, else NULL.
    5028              :  *
    5029              :  * The parser may hand back additional AlterTableCmd(s) and/or other
    5030              :  * utility statements, either before or after the original subcommand.
    5031              :  * Other AlterTableCmds are scheduled into the appropriate slot of the
    5032              :  * AlteredTableInfo (they had better be for later passes than the current one).
    5033              :  * Utility statements that are supposed to happen before the AlterTableCmd
    5034              :  * are executed immediately.  Those that are supposed to happen afterwards
    5035              :  * are added to the tab->afterStmts list to be done at the very end.
    5036              :  */

    5038           54 : ATParseTransformCmd(List **wqueue, AlteredTableInfo *tab, Relation rel,

    5045              :         List       *afterStmts;

    5048              :         /* Gin up an AlterTableStmt with just this subcommand and this table */

    5052              :                                          -1);
    5053           54 :         atstmt->relation->inh = recurse;
    5054           54 :         atstmt->cmds = list_make1(cmd);
    5055           54 :         atstmt->relkind = OBJECT_TABLE; /* needn't be picky here */
    5056           54 :         atstmt->missing_ok = false;
    5057              : 
    5058              :         /* Transform the AlterTableStmt */
    5059           54 :         atstmt = transformAlterTableStmt(RelationGetRelid(rel),
    5060              :                                                                          atstmt,
    5061              :                                                                          context->queryString,
    5062              :                                                                          &beforeStmts,
    5063              :                                                                          &afterStmts);
    5064              : 
    5065              :         /* Execute any statements that should happen before these subcommand(s) */

    5068            1 :                 Node       *stmt = (Node *) lfirst(lc);
    5069              : 
    5070            1 :                 ProcessUtilityForAlterTable(stmt, context);

    5073              : 
    5074              :         /* Examine the transformed subcommands and schedule them appropriately */

    5079              : 
    5080              :                 /*

    5084              :                  * original subcommand.  (Note, however, that this does cause

    5097            3 :                                 pass = AT_PASS_COL_ATTRS;
    5098            3 :                                 break;
    5099            0 :                         case AT_AddIndex:
    5100              :                                 /* This command never recurses */
    5101              :                                 /* No command-specific prep needed */
    5102            0 :                                 pass = AT_PASS_ADD_INDEX;
    5103            0 :                                 break;
    5104            3 :                         case AT_AddIndexConstraint:
    5105              :                                 /* This command never recurses */
    5106              :                                 /* No command-specific prep needed */

    5108            3 :                                 break;

    5110              :                                 /* Recursion occurs during execution phase */

    5125            0 :                         case AT_AlterColumnGenericOptions:

    5169              : /*
    5170              :  * ATRewriteTables: ALTER TABLE phase 3
    5171              :  */
    5172              : static void
    5173           52 : ATRewriteTables(AlterTableStmt *parsetree, List **wqueue, LOCKMODE lockmode,
    5174              :                                 AlterTableUtilityContext *context)
    5175              : {
    5176              :         ListCell   *ltab;
    5177              : 
    5178              :         /* Go through each table that needs to be checked or rewritten */
    5179          120 :         foreach(ltab, *wqueue)
    5180              :         {
    5181           69 :                 AlteredTableInfo *tab = (AlteredTableInfo *) lfirst(ltab);
    5182              : 
    5183              :                 /* Relations without storage may be ignored here */
    5184           69 :                 if (!RELKIND_HAS_STORAGE(tab->relkind))
    5185           38 :                         continue;
    5186              : 
    5187              :                 /*
    5188              :                  * If we change column data types or add/remove OIDs, the operation
    5189              :                  * has to be propagated to tables that use this table's rowtype as a
    5190              :                  * column type.  tab->newvals will also be non-NULL in the case where
    5191              :                  * we're adding a column with a default.  We choose to forbid that
    5192              :                  * case as well, since composite types might eventually support
    5193              :                  * defaults.
    5194              :                  *
    5195              :                  * (Eventually we'll probably need to check for composite type
    5196              :                  * dependencies even when we're just scanning the table without a
    5197              :                  * rewrite, but at the moment a composite type does not enforce any
    5198              :                  * constraints, so it's not necessary/appropriate to enforce them just
    5199              :                  * during ALTER.)
    5200              :                  */
    5201           31 :                 if (tab->newvals != NIL || tab->rewrite > 0)
    5202              :                 {
    5203              :                         Relation        rel;
    5204              : 
    5205            1 :                         rel = table_open(tab->relid, NoLock);
    5206            1 :                         find_composite_type_dependencies(rel->rd_rel->reltype, rel, NULL);
    5207            1 :                         table_close(rel, NoLock);
    5208              :                 }
    5209              : 
    5210              :                 /*
    5211              :                  * We only need to rewrite the table if at least one column needs to
    5212              :                  * be recomputed, we are adding/removing the OID column, or we are
    5213              :                  * changing its persistence.
    5214              :                  *
    5215              :                  * There are two reasons for requiring a rewrite when changing
    5216              :                  * persistence: on one hand, we need to ensure that the buffers
    5217              :                  * belonging to each of the two relations are marked with or without
    5218              :                  * BM_PERMANENT properly.  On the other hand, since rewriting creates
    5219              :                  * and assigns a new relfilenode, we automatically create or drop an
    5220              :                  * init fork for the relation as appropriate.
    5221              :                  */
    5222           31 :                 if (tab->rewrite > 0)
    5223              :                 {
    5224              :                         /* Build a temporary relation and copy data */
    5225              :                         Relation        OldHeap;
    5226              :                         Oid                     OIDNewHeap;
    5227              :                         Oid                     NewTableSpace;
    5228              :                         char            persistence;
    5229              : 
    5230            1 :                         OldHeap = table_open(tab->relid, NoLock);
    5231              : 
    5232              :                         /*
    5233              :                          * We don't support rewriting of system catalogs; there are too
    5234              :                          * many corner cases and too little benefit.  In particular this
    5235              :                          * is certainly not going to work for mapped catalogs.
    5236              :                          */
    5237            1 :                         if (IsSystemRelation(OldHeap))
    5238            0 :                                 ereport(ERROR,
    5239              :                                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
    5240              :                                                  errmsg("cannot rewrite system relation \"%s\"",
    5241              :                                                                 RelationGetRelationName(OldHeap))));
    5242              : 
    5243            1 :                         if (RelationIsUsedAsCatalogTable(OldHeap))
    5244            0 :                                 ereport(ERROR,
    5245              :                                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
    5246              :                                                  errmsg("cannot rewrite table \"%s\" used as a catalog table",
    5247              :                                                                 RelationGetRelationName(OldHeap))));
    5248              : 
    5249              :                         /*
    5250              :                          * Don't allow rewrite on temp tables of other backends ... their
    5251              :                          * local buffer manager is not going to cope.  (This is redundant
    5252              :                          * with the check in CheckAlterTableIsSafe, but for safety we'll
    5253              :                          * check here too.)
    5254              :                          */
    5255            1 :                         if (RELATION_IS_OTHER_TEMP(OldHeap))
    5256            0 :                                 ereport(ERROR,
    5257              :                                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
    5258              :                                                  errmsg("cannot rewrite temporary tables of other sessions")));
    5259              : 
    5260              :                         /*
    5261              :                          * Select destination tablespace (same as original unless user
    5262              :                          * requested a change)
    5263              :                          */
    5264            1 :                         if (tab->newTableSpace)
    5265            0 :                                 NewTableSpace = tab->newTableSpace;
    5266              :                         else
    5267            1 :                                 NewTableSpace = OldHeap->rd_rel->reltablespace;
    5268              : 
    5269              :                         /*
    5270              :                          * Select persistence of transient table (same as original unless
    5271              :                          * user requested a change)
    5272              :                          */
    5273            1 :                         persistence = tab->chgPersistence ?
    5274            1 :                                 tab->newrelpersistence : OldHeap->rd_rel->relpersistence;
    5275              : 
    5276            1 :                         table_close(OldHeap, NoLock);
    5277              : 
    5278              :                         /*
    5279              :                          * Fire off an Event Trigger now, before actually rewriting the
    5280              :                          * table.
    5281              :                          *
    5282              :                          * We don't support Event Trigger for nested commands anywhere,
    5283              :                          * here included, and parsetree is given NULL when coming from
    5284              :                          * AlterTableInternal.
    5285              :                          *
    5286              :                          * And fire it only once.
    5287              :                          */
    5288            1 :                         if (parsetree)
    5289            1 :                                 EventTriggerTableRewrite((Node *) parsetree,
    5290              :                                                                                  tab->relid,
    5291              :                                                                                  tab->rewrite);
    5292              : 
    5293              :                         /*
    5294              :                          * Create transient table that will receive the modified data.
    5295              :                          *

    5299              :                          * that the original filenode's buffers will get read in with the

    5301              :                          * after the rewrite would possibly result with buffers for the
    5302              :                          * original filenode having the wrong persistence setting.

    6066              : /*
    6067              :  * find_composite_type_dependencies
    6068              :  *
    6069              :  * Check to see if the type "typeOid" is being used as a column in some table
    6070              :  * (possibly nested several levels deep in composite types, arrays, etc!).
    6071              :  * Eventually, we'd like to propagate the check or rewrite operation
    6072              :  * into such tables, but for now, just error out if we find any.

    6075              :  * or a type name (not both) for use in the error message, if any.
    6076              :  *
    6077              :  * Note that "typeOid" is not necessarily a composite type; it could also be
    6078              :  * another container type such as an array or range, or a domain over one of
    6079              :  * these things.  The name of this function is therefore somewhat historical,
    6080              :  * but it's not worth changing.

    6083              :  * to reject the ALTER.  (How safe is this really?)

    6093              : 
    6094              :         /* since this function recurses, it could be driven to stack overflow */
    6095            2 :         check_stack_depth();
    6096              : 
    6097              :         /*

    6101            2 :         depRel = table_open(DependRelationId, AccessShareLock);
    6102              : 
    6103            2 :         ScanKeyInit(&key[0],
    6104              :                                 Anum_pg_depend_refclassid,
    6105              :                                 BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,
    6106              :                                 ObjectIdGetDatum(TypeRelationId));
    6107            2 :         ScanKeyInit(&key[1],
    6108              :                                 Anum_pg_depend_refobjid,
    6109              :                                 BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,
    6110              :                                 ObjectIdGetDatum(typeOid));
    6111              : 
    6112            2 :         depScan = systable_beginscan(depRel, DependReferenceIndexId, true,
    6113              :                                                                  NULL, 2, key);
    6114              : 
    6115            3 :         while (HeapTupleIsValid(depTup = systable_getnext(depScan)))
    6116              :         {
    6117            1 :                 Form_pg_depend pg_depend = (Form_pg_depend) GETSTRUCT(depTup);
    6118              :                 Relation        rel;
    6119              :                 TupleDesc       tupleDesc;
    6120              :                 Form_pg_attribute att;
    6121              : 
    6122              :                 /* Check for directly dependent types */
    6123            1 :                 if (pg_depend->classid == TypeRelationId)
    6124              :                 {

    6132              :                                                                                          origRelation, origTypeName);
    6133            1 :                         continue;
    6134              :                 }
    6135              : 
    6136              :                 /* Else, ignore dependees that aren't relations */
    6137            0 :                 if (pg_depend->classid != RelationRelationId)
    6138            0 :                         continue;
    6139              : 
    6140            0 :                 rel = relation_open(pg_depend->objid, AccessShareLock);
    6141            0 :                 tupleDesc = RelationGetDescr(rel);
    6142              : 
    6143              :                 /*
    6144              :                  * If objsubid identifies a specific column, refer to that in error
    6145              :                  * messages.  Otherwise, search to see if there's a user column of the
    6146              :                  * type.  (We assume system columns are never of interesting types.)
    6147              :                  * The search is needed because an index containing an expression
    6148              :                  * column of the target type will just be recorded as a whole-relation
    6149              :                  * dependency.  If we do not find a column of the type, the dependency
    6150              :                  * must indicate that the type is transiently referenced in an index
    6151              :                  * expression but not stored on disk, which we assume is OK, just as
    6152              :                  * we do for references in views.  (It could also be that the target
    6153              :                  * type is embedded in some container type that is stored in an index
    6154              :                  * column, but the previous recursion should catch such cases.)
    6155              :                  */
    6156            0 :                 if (pg_depend->objsubid > 0 && pg_depend->objsubid <= tupleDesc->natts)

    6159              :                 {
    6160            0 :                         att = NULL;
    6161            0 :                         for (int attno = 1; attno <= tupleDesc->natts; attno++)

    6163            0 :                                 att = TupleDescAttr(tupleDesc, attno - 1);

    6169              :                         {
    6170              :                                 /* No such column, so assume OK */
    6171            0 :                                 relation_close(rel, AccessShareLock);

    6173              :                         }

    6237              : /*
    6238              :  * find_typed_table_dependencies
    6239              :  *
    6240              :  * Check to see if a composite type is being used as the type of a

    6244              : static List *
    6245            0 : find_typed_table_dependencies(Oid typeOid, const char *typeName, DropBehavior behavior)

    6260            0 :         scan = table_beginscan_catalog(classRel, 1, key);

    6265              : 
    6266            0 :                 if (behavior == DROP_RESTRICT)

    6278              :

    6282              : 
    6283              : /*
    6284              :  * check_of_type
    6285              :  *
    6286              :  * Check whether a type is suitable for CREATE TABLE OF/ALTER TABLE OF.  If it
    6287              :  * isn't suitable, throw an error.  Currently, we require that the type
    6288              :  * originated with CREATE TYPE AS.  We could support any row type, but doing so
    6289              :  * would require handling a number of extra corner cases in the DDL commands.
    6290              :  * (Also, allowing domain-over-composite would open up a can of worms about
    6291              :  * whether and how the domain's constraints should apply to derived tables.)
    6292              :  */
    6293              : void
    6294            0 : check_of_type(HeapTuple typetuple)
    6295              : {
    6296            0 :         Form_pg_type typ = (Form_pg_type) GETSTRUCT(typetuple);
    6297            0 :         bool            typeOk = false;
    6298              : 
    6299            0 :         if (typ->typtype == TYPTYPE_COMPOSITE)
    6300              :         {
    6301              :                 Relation        typeRelation;
    6302              : 
    6303              :                 Assert(OidIsValid(typ->typrelid));

    6322              : /*

    7233              :                                  errhint("Do not specify the ONLY keyword.")));

    7235            0 :         ReleaseSysCache(tuple);

    7238              : /*
    7239              :  * NotNullImpliedByRelConstraints

    7242              : static bool
    7243            2 : NotNullImpliedByRelConstraints(Relation rel, Form_pg_attribute attr)

    7624              :          * has a generation expression that the parent does not have, the child
    7625              :          * column will necessarily be an attlocal column.  So to implement ONLY
    7626              :          * here, we'd need extra code to update attislocal of the direct child

    8542              : /*

    8555           35 : ChooseForeignKeyConstraintNameAddition(List *colnames)

    8566           36 :                 if (buflen > 0)
    8567            1 :                         buf[buflen++] = '_';    /* insert _ between names */
    8568              :

    8576            0 :                         break;
    8577              :         }
    8578           35 :         return pstrdup(buf);
    8579              : }

    9067              :                         /*

    9085              :                          * type.  Since the constraint was previously valid, all values on
    9086              :                          * the foreign side necessarily exist on the primary side and in

    9088              :                          * domains specially here.

    9092              :                          * equality), we don't compare collation here.

    9095              :                          * binary coercible to the opcintype of the unique index column,

    9145              : 
    9146              : /*
    9147              :  * addFkRecurseReferenced
    9148              :  *              subroutine for ATAddForeignKeyConstraint; recurses on the referenced
    9149              :  *              side of the constraint
    9150              :  *
    9151              :  * Create pg_constraint rows for the referenced side of the constraint,
    9152              :  * referencing the parent of the referencing side; also create action triggers
    9153              :  * on leaf partitions.  If the table is partitioned, recurse to handle each
    9154              :  * partition.
    9155              :  *
    9156              :  * wqueue is the ALTER TABLE work queue; can be NULL when not running as part
    9157              :  * of an ALTER TABLE sequence.
    9158              :  * fkconstraint is the constraint being added.
    9159              :  * rel is the root referencing relation.
    9160              :  * pkrel is the referenced relation; might be a partition, if recursing.
    9161              :  * indexOid is the OID of the index (on pkrel) implementing this constraint.
    9162              :  * parentConstr is the OID of a parent constraint; InvalidOid if this is a
    9163              :  * top-level constraint.
    9164              :  * numfks is the number of columns in the foreign key
    9165              :  * pkattnum is the attnum array of referenced attributes.
    9166              :  * fkattnum is the attnum array of referencing attributes.
    9167              :  * pf/pp/ffeqoperators are OID array of operators between columns.
    9168              :  * old_check_ok signals that this constraint replaces an existing one that
    9169              :  * was already validated (thus this one doesn't need validation).
    9170              :  */
    9171              : static ObjectAddress
    9172           37 : addFkRecurseReferenced(List **wqueue, Constraint *fkconstraint, Relation rel,
    9173              :                                            Relation pkrel, Oid indexOid, Oid parentConstr,
    9174              :                                            int numfks,
    9175              :                                            int16 *pkattnum, int16 *fkattnum, Oid *pfeqoperators,
    9176              :                                            Oid *ppeqoperators, Oid *ffeqoperators, bool old_check_ok)
    9177              : {
    9178              :         ObjectAddress address;
    9179              :         Oid                     constrOid;
    9180              :         char       *conname;
    9181              :         bool            conislocal;
    9182              :         int                     coninhcount;
    9183              :         bool            connoinherit;
    9184              : 
    9185              :         /*
    9186              :          * Verify relkind for each referenced partition.  At the top level, this
    9187              :          * is redundant with a previous check, but we need it when recursing.
    9188              :          */
    9189           37 :         if (pkrel->rd_rel->relkind != RELKIND_RELATION &&
    9190            8 :                 pkrel->rd_rel->relkind != RELKIND_PARTITIONED_TABLE)
    9191            0 :                 ereport(ERROR,
    9192              :                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_WRONG_OBJECT_TYPE),
    9193              :                                  errmsg("referenced relation \"%s\" is not a table",
    9194              :                                                 RelationGetRelationName(pkrel))));
    9195              : 
    9196              :         /*
    9197              :          * Caller supplies us with a constraint name; however, it may be used in
    9198              :          * this partition, so come up with a different one in that case.
    9199              :          */
    9200           37 :         if (ConstraintNameIsUsed(CONSTRAINT_RELATION,
    9201              :                                                          RelationGetRelid(rel),
    9202           37 :                                                          fkconstraint->conname))
    9203           17 :                 conname = ChooseConstraintName(RelationGetRelationName(rel),
    9204           17 :                                                                            ChooseForeignKeyConstraintNameAddition(fkconstraint->fk_attrs),
    9205              :                                                                            "fkey",
    9206           17 :                                                                            RelationGetNamespace(rel), NIL);
    9207              :         else
    9208           20 :                 conname = fkconstraint->conname;
    9209              : 
    9210           37 :         if (OidIsValid(parentConstr))
    9211              :         {
    9212           17 :                 conislocal = false;
    9213           17 :                 coninhcount = 1;
    9214           17 :                 connoinherit = false;
    9215              :         }
    9216              :         else
    9217              :         {
    9218           20 :                 conislocal = true;
    9219           20 :                 coninhcount = 0;
    9220              : 
    9221              :                 /*
    9222              :                  * always inherit for partitioned tables, never for legacy inheritance
    9223              :                  */
    9224           20 :                 connoinherit = rel->rd_rel->relkind != RELKIND_PARTITIONED_TABLE;

    9347              : /*
    9348              :  * addFkRecurseReferencing
    9349              :  *              subroutine for ATAddForeignKeyConstraint and CloneFkReferencing
    9350              :  *
    9351              :  * If the referencing relation is a plain relation, create the necessary check
    9352              :  * triggers that implement the constraint, and set up for Phase 3 constraint
    9353              :  * verification.  If the referencing relation is a partitioned table, then
    9354              :  * we create a pg_constraint row for it and recurse on this routine for each
    9355              :  * partition.
    9356              :  *
    9357              :  * We assume that the referenced relation is locked against concurrent
    9358              :  * deletions.  If it's a partitioned relation, every partition must be so
    9359              :  * locked.
    9360              :  *
    9361              :  * wqueue is the ALTER TABLE work queue; can be NULL when not running as part
    9362              :  * of an ALTER TABLE sequence.
    9363              :  * fkconstraint is the constraint being added.
    9364              :  * rel is the referencing relation; might be a partition, if recursing.
    9365              :  * pkrel is the root referenced relation.
    9366              :  * indexOid is the OID of the index (on pkrel) implementing this constraint.
    9367              :  * parentConstr is the OID of the parent constraint (there is always one).
    9368              :  * numfks is the number of columns in the foreign key
    9369              :  * pkattnum is the attnum array of referenced attributes.
    9370              :  * fkattnum is the attnum array of referencing attributes.
    9371              :  * pf/pp/ffeqoperators are OID array of operators between columns.
    9372              :  * old_check_ok signals that this constraint replaces an existing one that
    9373              :  *              was already validated (thus this one doesn't need validation).
    9374              :  * lockmode is the lockmode to acquire on partitions when recursing.
    9375              :  */

    9403              :
